results.seo_title: NVIDIA and SAP Bring Trust to Specialized Agents
results.meta_description: NVIDIA and SAP collaborate to bring security and governance controls to specialized AI agents, addressing the trust equation in enterprise systems.
results.body_html:
NVIDIA and SAP have collaborated to bring security and governance controls to specialized AI agents, addressing the trust equation in enterprise systems.

Expanded Collaboration for Security and Governance

SAP embeds NVIDIA OpenShell into SAP Business AI Platform, providing isolated execution environments and policy enforcement at the filesystem and network layers. NVIDIA engineers are codesigning OpenShell alongside SAP engineers, contributing back to the open source project. OpenShell provides infrastructure-level containment that guards against damage when agent logic fails.

Addressing the Trust Equation in Enterprise Systems

NVIDIA OpenShell Provides isolated execution environments and policy enforcement at the filesystem and network layers.

SAP Business AI Platform Embeds NVIDIA OpenShell, providing a runtime security layer for all SAP AI agents.

Security and Governance Controls Address the trust equation in enterprise systems by ensuring secure development and deployment of specialized AI agents.

Improved security and governance controls for specialized AI agents Increased confidence in the use of autonomous AI agents in enterprise systems Enhanced data protection and reduced risk of damage when agent logic fails

NVIDIA and SAP will continue to collaborate on open source projects, such as OpenShell. Enterprises can leverage NVIDIA OpenShell and SAP Business AI Platform to develop and deploy secure specialized AI agents.

source.title: NVIDIA and SAP Bring Trust to Specialized Agents
source.summary: Announced today at SAP Sapphire — where NVIDIA founder and CEO Jensen Huang joined  SAP CEO Christian Klein’s keynote by video — SAP and NVIDIA’s expanded collaboration helps enterprises run specialized agents with security and governance controls.

results.seo_title: Meta Supervision Tools Give Parents Insights into Their Teen's…
results.meta_description: New supervision tools from Meta provide parents with insights into their teen's algorithm on Instagram and more.
results.body_html:
Parents can now view the general topics their teens engage with using Meta's supervision tools.

Viewing Algorithm Topics

  • Parents and guardians can access this feature in Family Center on Instagram, Facebook, and Messenger.
  • The tool provides a clearer understanding of the content their teens see on Instagram.
  • By expanding these insights to cover all available categories within Your Algorithm, families have a broader view of what their teens are engaging with.

Customizing Algorithm Settings

SettingDescription
Topic filtersParents can choose to see or hide specific topics in their teen's algorithm.
Content preferencesParents can select content types, such as photos, videos, or stories, to display in their teen's feed.

Notifications for Algorithm Changes

Soon, parents will receive notifications when their teen adds a new interest to their algorithm, helping explain why their content may change over time.

For example, if a teen adds an interest like basketball, photography, or musicals, their algorithm may adjust accordingly. Supervision gives parents broader visibility into those changes.

This update builds on existing protections like Teen Accounts on Instagram, Facebook, and Messenger, which include automatic privacy settings, limits on who can contact teens, and tools to help manage the content they see.

source.title: New Supervision Tools Give Parents Insights Into Their Teen’s Algorithm and More
source.summary:
We’re making it easier for parents to manage and supervise their teen’s experiences across our apps.

The post New Supervision Tools Give Parents Insights Into Their Teen’s Algorithm and More appeared first on Meta Newsroom.

results.seo_title: Neutralizing the Gigascale Problem: How to Solve the Physical Power…
results.meta_description: Discover how Ampace's semi-solid-state battery innovation and Eaton's system intelligence can neutralize the power paradox in gigascale AI training loads.
results.body_html:
The real bottleneck in gigascale AI training loads is not the thermal limit of the chip or the capacity of the cooling system, but the dynamic resilience of the power chain.

Conventional power systems were designed for steady-state loads, not the rapid heartbeat of a massive AI GPU cluster. Ampace's semi-solid and low-electrolyte cells address this challenge by acting as high-speed 'shock absorbers'. Leveraging ultra-low internal resistance (DCR) and high cycle capability, these batteries neutralize millisecond-level power spikes at the source, stabilizing the local power loop before disturbances propagate upstream to the grid or on-site generators.

Key FeaturesDescriptionRamp-Rate ControlEaton's double-conversion topologies and advanced power electronics upgrades enable rapid load responsiveness and high system stability.Average Power ManagementComplementary algorithmic approaches in modern UPS platforms suppress sub-synchronous oscillations and optimize load smoothing.Semi-Solid State ChemistryAmpace's semi-solid state chemistry minimizes liquid electrolyte, greatly reducing the risk of leakage and thermal runaway under continuous AI high-load conditions.

The industry has explored various mitigations — from rack-level BBUs to 800V DC architectures — yet the mature, high volume, traditional UPS system remains the most viable and scalable foundation for gigawatt-level facilities. Consequently, the UPS-integrated battery system has emerged as the critical 'physical buffer' to neutralize these pulses at the source.

The power usage of these gigascale sites and their drastic, high frequency, abrupt load surges from the AI GPU clusters can trigger transient voltage events and frequency instability, risking the entire local grid. The grid itself is not robust enough to support these loads. This leads to the infrastructure gap: The utility is not robust enough and traditional backup sources, such as diesel generators and gas turbines, simply cannot react to millisecond-level power spikes in output.

By transforming energy storage from passive 'standby insurance' into active, schedulable assets, the system simultaneously safeguards continuous AI training and maintains the long-term health of the data center infrastructure. In practical terms, this means that even during peak compute bursts, the infrastructure remains stable, training cycles continue uninterrupted, and operators avoid costly oversizing or grid stress.

The 'safety-first' approach ensures that infrastructure can sustain aggressive performance targets without compromising the physical integrity of the facility. Coupled with over a decade of proven high-cycle life operation and design under shallow pulse conditions, these systems can extend operational lifespan, reduce replacement requirements, and provide operators with confidence that safety and reliability remain uncompromised as compute density continues to grow.

source.title: Neutralizing the Gigascale Problem: How to Solve the Physical Power Paradox of Extreme AI Training Loads
source.summary:
This sponsored article is brought to you by Ampace.

As AI workloads grow to gigascale levels, the global data center industry has hit a hidden physical wall. The real bottleneck is no longer just the thermal limit of the chip or the capacity of the cooling system — it is the dynamic resilience of the power chain.

Modern AI computing clusters, driven by massive GPU clusters, generate high-frequency, abrupt, and synchronized spikey pulse loads. As rack densities soar beyond 100 kW, these fluctuations are amplified into a “power paradox”: while the digital logic of AI is moving faster than ever, the physical infrastructure supporting it remains tethered to legacy response capabilities.

The power usage of these gigascale sites and their drastic, high frequency, abrupt load surges from the AI GPU clusters can trigger transient voltage events and frequency instability, risking the entire local grid. The grid itself is not robust enough to support these loads. This leads to the infrastructure gap: The utility is not robust enough and traditional backup sources, such as diesel generators and gas turbines, simply cannot react to millisecond-level power spikes in output. This will often force operators into a cycle of costly infrastructure over sizing just to buffer the volatility.

AI infrastructure requires energy systems capable of instantaneous response while safeguarding continuity and reliability.

The industry has explored various mitigations — from rack-level BBUs to 800V DC architectures — yet the mature, high volume, traditional UPS system remains the most viable and scalable foundation for gigawatt-lev

results.seo_title: Meta Celebrates African Cinema With 'Made by Africa, Loved by the…
results.meta_description: Meta launches sixth edition of its pan-African campaign, 'Made by Africa, loved by the world: Where stories spark community,' featuring a five-part vodcast…
results.body_html:
Africa Day is celebrated on May 25, and this year, Facebook has launched the sixth edition of its pan-African campaign, ‘Made by Africa, loved by the world: Where stories spark community,’ featuring a five-part vodcast series profiling internationally acclaimed actors and filmmakers from Nigeria and South Africa.

Featured Talents

  • Kehinde Bankole (Nigeria) – Two-time AMVCA winner; performed at Shakespeare’s Globe Theatre; voiced Disney’s Iwaju; starred in Netflix and Amazon Prime productions.
  • Linda Mtoba (South Africa) – Award-winning actress known for Isibaya, The River, and BET’s Queendom; digital entrepreneur and presenter.
  • Nomzamo Mbatha (South Africa) – TIME 100 Next honoree, UNHCR Goodwill Ambassador, and WEF Young Global Leader; starred in Coming 2 America and Shaka iLembe; executive producer and founder of the Nomzamo Lighthouse Foundation.
  • Osas Ighodaro (Nigeria) – Two-time AMVCA Best Actress winner; Nigerian-American actress and producer known for The Smart Money Woman and Fela and the Kalakuta Queens; founder of Imuenyan Productions.
  • Tobi Bakre (Nigeria) – AMVCA-nominated actor known for Brotherhood and Prime Video’s Gangs of Lagos; one of Nollywood’s fastest-rising leading men.

Partnership with Amplify Africa

Africa Day is about more than a moment in history, it’s a celebration of who we are, and the impact African culture continues to have on the world. For ten years, Amplify Africa has been dedicated to telling that story, so partnering with Meta on this Made by Africa Campaign feels like a natural fit.

“Africa Day is about more than a moment in history, it’s a celebration of who we are, and the impact African culture continues to have on the world. For ten years, Amplify Africa has been dedicated to telling that story, so partnering with Meta on this Made by Africa Campaign feels like a natural fit.” – Dami Kujembola, CEO & Co-Founder, Amplify Africa

Community Challenge #ShareYourAfrica

The campaign also evolves its community challenge, #ShareYourAfrica, inviting creators across lifestyle, fashion, and dance to use Meta AI to celebrate African culture through Reels showcasing the traditions and moments shaping African identity today.

PlatformDescription
Meta Africa Facebook pageVodcast snippets will be available on this platform.
Podcasters’ channelsFull episodes of the vodcast series will be available on these platforms.
Talent profilesProfiles of the featured talents will be available on this platform.

source.title: Celebrating African Cinema With the ‘Made by Africa, Loved by the World’ 2026 Campaign
source.summary: In celebration of Africa Day on May 25, Facebook has launched the sixth edition of its pan-African campaign, ‘Made by Africa, loved by the world: Where stories spark community.’ This year’s campaign shifts the spotlight to African cinema.  The 2026 campaign features a five-part vodcast series profiling five internationally acclaimed actors and filmmakers from Nigeria and South Africa, hosted by leading African podcasters I Said What I Said (Nigeria) and Because We Said So (South Africa). “For six years, Made by Africa has spotlighted talent from across the continent making a mark globally. This year, film takes centre stage. From Nollywood to South African cinema, African stories are reaching audiences worldwide, and Facebook is at the heart of how people come together around cultural moments like these. This campaign backs the filmmakers driving that momentum.” said, Kezia Anim-Addo, Communications Director, Africa, Middle East & Turkey The featured talents: Kehinde Bankole (Nigeria) – Two-time AMVCA winner; performed at Shakespeare’s Globe Theatre; voiced Disney’s Iwaju; starred in Netflix and Amazon Prime productions. results.seo_title: Archivists Turn to LLMs to Decipher Handwriting at Scale
results.meta_description: General-purpose AI models are now good enough to change what archives can do, enabling scholars and families to ask questions they rarely had the time or…
results.body_html:
General-purpose AI models are now good enough to change what archives can do, enabling scholars and families to ask questions they rarely had the time or money to ask before.

Unlocking Hidden Archives

  • Pages that once required paleography training, custom software, or weeks of squinting can produce usable transcriptions in seconds.
  • Collections that were preserved but functionally hidden are becoming searchable, opening up the ability for scholars and families to ask questions they rarely had the time or money to ask before.

Scaling AI to Decipher Archival Handwriting

Benchmarking ResultsComparison with Transkribus
Accuracy: 2% vs 8%Speed: 50x faster
Cost: 1/50th the cost

Practical Consequences

"The dream was to have something like what we have now," says Mark Humphries, a professor of history and coordinator of the applied generative AI program at Wilfrid Laurier University.

Lianne Leddy, an associate professor of history and the Canada Research Chair in Indigenous Histories and Historical Practice, traces Indigenous women’s experiences across North America through fur trade records and other archival materials.

source.title: Archivists Turn to LLMs to Decipher Handwriting at Scale
source.summary:
When I sat down with bell hooks’ personal journals at an archive at Berea College in Kentucky, I expected an intimate peek into her private thoughts, her voice before the editing. What I got instead was frustration. Her handwriting was dense cursive, all loops that looked identical to my eye, and there were years of journals to go through. I found myself photographing pages and feeding them to ChatGPT just to read what she’d written. My tool of choice worked well, and it turns out I’m not the first person in an archive to have figured this out.

Getting computers to reliably read human handwriting, in all its variations, has challenged AI researchers since the earliest days of the field. Researchers in the 1960s predicted machines would soon simply devour handwritten text; instead the problem spawned decades of specialized research and entire commercial industries. Yann LeCun, who later went on to win the Turing Award for his contributions to deep learning, published landmark work on handwritten digit recognition in the 1980s that showed what was possible in narrow, controlled settings. Real archives were another matter.

Now that boundary is moving. General-purpose AI models are not perfect readers of every handwritten page, but they are now suddenly good enough to change what archives can do. Pages that once required paleography training, custom software, or weeks of squinting can produce usable transcriptions in seconds. Collections that were preserved but functionally hidden are becoming searchable, opening up the ability for scholars and families to ask questions they rarely had the time or money to ask before.

results.seo_title: NVIDIA's Hermes Unlocks Self-Improving AI Agents
results.meta_description: Hermes Agent, powered by NVIDIA RTX PCs and DGX Spark, is a self-improving AI agent that crosses 140,000 GitHub stars in under three months.
results.body_html:
Hermes Agent, powered by NVIDIA RTX PCs and DGX Spark, is a self-improving AI agent that crosses 140,000 GitHub stars in under three months.

Self-Evolving Skills: Hermes writes and refines its own skills every time it encounters a complex task or receives feedback. Contained Sub-Agents: Hermes treats sub-agents as short-lived, isolated workers dedicated to a sub-task with a focused context and set of tools. Reliability by design: Nous Research curates and stress-tests every skill, tool, and plug-in that ships with Hermes.

NVIDIA RTX PCs/DGX SparkAcceleration for Agentic AI

NVIDIA RTX PCs/DGX Spark Accelerated agentic AI with high-performance, open-weight large language models (LLMs)

Qwen 3.6 LLMs for Hermes Qwen 3.6 27B and 35B parameter models outperform previous-generation 120B and 400B parameter model counterparts, running on NVIDIA RTX and DGX Spark.

source.title: Hermes Unlocks Self-Improving AI Agents, Powered by NVIDIA RTX PCs and DGX Spark
source.summary: Agentic AI is changing the way users get work done. Following the success of OpenClaw, the community is embracing new open source agentic frameworks. The latest is Hermes Agent, which crossed 140,000 GitHub stars in under three months.

results.seo_title: NVIDIA and Ineffable Intelligence Collaborate on Reinforcement…
results.meta_description: NVIDIA and Ineffable Intelligence team up to build the future of reinforcement learning infrastructure, enabling superlearners that learn continuously from…
results.body_html:
NVIDIA and Ineffable Intelligence are collaborating to build the future of reinforcement learning infrastructure. This partnership aims to enable superlearners that learn continuously from experience, a crucial step towards developing more advanced AI systems.

Key Features of the Reinforcement Learning Infrastructure

NVIDIA's expertise in high-performance computing and Ineffable Intelligence's knowledge in reinforcement learning will come together to create a powerful pipeline for large-scale reinforcement learning. The system will be designed to learn from experience, acting, observing, scoring, and updating continuously in tight loops. OpenAlex: A present to Mizar on its 50th anniversary, this AI/TP system automatically proves about 60% of the Mizar theorems in the hammer setting.

The partnership will focus on building a new paradigm for reinforcement learning, enabling systems that discover new knowledge for themselves. The system will be optimized to support continuous learning from experience, addressing the harder problem of AI: how to build systems that discover new knowledge.

The system will require novel model architectures and training algorithms to handle rich forms of experience distinct from human language and data. The partnership will need to address the challenges of interconnect, memory bandwidth, and serving in tight loops.

The partnership will explore ways to implement indicator properties of consciousness in AI systems, assessing their ability to learn from experience. The system will be designed to learn continuously from experience, enabling superlearners that can adapt and improve over time.

source.title: NVIDIA, Ineffable Intelligence Team Up to Build the Future of Reinforcement Learning Infrastructure
source.summary: Reinforcement-learning agents — AI systems that learn by trial and error — can convert computation into new knowledge. That’s the focus of a new engineering-level collaboration between NVIDIA and Ineffable Intelligence, the London-based AI lab founded by AlphaGo architect David Silver in the wake of Ineffable’s emergence from stealth last week. “The next frontier of […]

results.seo_title: Can AI Chatbots Reason Like Doctors? OpenAI's LLM Outperforms…
results.meta_description: A recent study published in Science found that a large language model from OpenAI outperformed physicians on several clinical reasoning tasks using real…
results.body_html:
Yes, according to a recent study published in Science, a large language model from OpenAI has outperformed physicians on several clinical reasoning tasks using real emergency room records.

Key Findings

  • The performance of OpenAI's o1-preview, a general-purpose model, was promising enough for the authors to recommend further testing of LLMs in real-life cases.
  • The study used real emergency room records and compared the performance of the LLM with two physicians on diagnostic tasks at multiple stages of care.
  • The LLM consistently edged out the humans, providing an exact or very close diagnosis 82% of the time at the final checkpoint.

Limitations and Future Directions

The study highlights the need for more proof in prospective clinical trials to fully understand the benefits and risks of using LLMs in medical decision-making.

  • There is currently no agreed-upon standard scoring system in place for evaluating LLMs in clinical reasoning tasks.
  • Newer LLM models, or those trained specifically for medical use, might perform even better than the current model.

Regulation and Liability

The study raises important questions about regulation and liability regarding the use of AI in medicine.

“We don’t want to rain on the parade. We think responsible innovation is the way to go,” says Arya Rao, a researcher at Harvard’s Icahn School of Medicine.

source.title: Can AI Chatbots Reason Like Doctors?
source.summary:
One of the earliest stated goals for computing in medicine was to aid in clinical reasoning: the decision-making steps required to reach a diagnosis and form a treatment plan. And over the years, researchers have built many clinical decision support systems, which have typically been purpose-built, with painstakingly written rules about symptoms, test thresholds, and medication interactions. As artificial intelligence capabilities develop, clinical reasoning is a natural application.

Now, a large language model (LLM) from OpenAI has outperformed physicians on several clinical reasoning tasks using real emergency room records, according to a study published 30 April in Science.

The new findings arrive amid a wave of concerning evidence about medical information from chatbots, with some studies showing impressive diagnostic performance while others document fabricated citations, flawed advice, and results that shift depending on how researchers score the systems. Despite that uncertainty, products aimed towards medical professionals are already entering the market. For example, this year OpenAI introduced ChatGPT for Clinicians and ChatGPT for Healthcare.

The performance of OpenAI’s o1-preview, a general-purpose model that has since been supplanted by newer models, was promising enough for the authors to recommend further testing of LLMs in real life cases, with physicians seeking second opinions on diagn

results.seo_title: Meta Introduces Completely Private Way to Chat With AI
results.meta_description: Learn about Meta's Incognito Chat feature that allows private conversations with AI, and explore its benefits and limitations.
results.body_html:
Direct answer: Meta has introduced a completely private way to chat with AI, allowing users to have invisible conversations.

How Incognito Chat Works

  • Incognito Chat uses end-to-end encryption to ensure that only the user and Meta AI can access the conversation.
  • The feature is available on WhatsApp and other messaging platforms.
  • Users can switch between private and public modes at will.

Benefits of Incognito Chat

FeatureDescription
End-to-end encryptionEnsures that only the user and Meta AI can access the conversation.
Private modeAllows users to have invisible conversations.
Switchable modesUsers can switch between private and public modes at will.

Limitations of Incognito Chat

While Incognito Chat offers several benefits, it also has some limitations. For example:

Incognito Chat is only available on WhatsApp and other messaging platforms.

source.title: Introducing a Completely Private Way to Chat With AI
source.summary:
Incognito Chat lets you talk to Meta AI in a way that is invisible to anyone else.

The post Introducing a Completely Private Way to Chat With AI appeared first on Meta Newsroom.

results.seo_title: Meta Introduces Instants: A New Way to Share Photos in the Moment
results.meta_description: Meta introduces Instants, a new way to share photos in the moment with your Close Friends or mutual followers with just a tap.
results.body_html:
Photos you share on Instants disappear after they’ve been viewed and can’t be viewed after 24 hours.

You can capture an instant by tapping the mini pile of photos at the bottom right corner of your Instagram inbox or by opening the Instants app. From there, snap a photo in real time — no uploads from your phone’s photo gallery. You can also add a caption to your photo, but can’t make any further edits to it.

Features to Make Sharing With Friends Simple

Archive Your shared instants are saved in a private archive that only you can see for up to one year.

Recap to Stories Compile instants from your archive into a recap and post it to Instagram Stories for your followers.

No screenshots Friends can’t screenshot or record instants you share.

Undo Accidentally sent an instant? Quickly take it back before friends see it by tapping the undo button.

Snooze Hold down the pile of instants in your inbox to snooze them for later.

source.title: Introducing Instants: A New Way to Share in the Moment
source.summary:
Share authentic moments as they’re happening with your Close Friends or mutual followers with just a tap.

The post Introducing Instants: A New Way to Share in the Moment appeared first on Meta Newsroom.

results.seo_title: Google AI Misdirects Users to Share Personal Contact Info
results.meta_description: A recent incident highlights the risks of Google's generative AI, which can misdirect users into sharing their personal contact information.
results.body_html:
Yes, Google's generative AI can misdirect users into sharing their personal contact information.

Google's generative AI uses natural language processing to generate responses to user queries. The AI may not always understand the context of the query or the intent behind it. This can lead to misdirection, where users are directed to share sensitive information.

Use a VPN To mask IP addresses and prevent tracking. Reduces the risk of being tracked by Google's AI.

Verify sources To ensure that information is coming from a trusted source. Avoids sharing sensitive information with unverified sources.

Risks and Consequences Sharing personal contact information can have serious consequences, including identity theft and financial loss.

According to a recent report, there have been several incidents of Google's generative AI misdirecting users into sharing their personal contact information. Conclusion The incident highlights the need for greater caution when using Google's generative AI and emphasizes the importance of verifying sources before sharing sensitive information.

source.title: AI chatbots are giving out people’s real phone numbers
source.summary: A Redditor recently wrote that he was “desperate for help”: for about a month, he said, his phone had been inundated by calls from “strangers” who were “looking for a lawyer, a product designer, a locksmith.” Callers were apparently misdirected by Google’s generative AI.  In March, a software developer in Israel was contacted on WhatsApp…

results.seo_title: India's E-commerce Landscape Shifted by AI-Powered Trends
results.meta_description: India's e-commerce landscape is undergoing a fundamental shift driven by advances in AI, video-first discovery, and conversational messaging.
results.body_html:
India's e-commerce landscape is undergoing a fundamental shift driven by advances in AI, video-first discovery, and conversational messaging.

Key Trends Reshaping India's E-Commerce

AI-powered discovery and video-first commerce are transforming the shopping experience in India. The traditional search-and-transact model is giving way to a dynamic, scroll-led discovery ecosystem. Conversational messaging is driving transactions and changing the way people shop across the country.

Partnership Ads AI-powered creative tool for brands to create more effective campaigns.

GenAI-powered creative A tool that uses AI to generate creative content for brands.

Impact of AI on E-Commerce Jobs and Skills

The use of AI in e-commerce is creating new job opportunities in areas such as data analysis and marketing. Brands need to develop skills in AI-powered tools and platforms to stay competitive.

Conclusion India's e-commerce landscape is shifting towards a more dynamic and interactive shopping experience, driven by advances in AI, video-first discovery, and conversational messaging.

source.title: From Scroll to Chat to Cart: Trends Reshaping How India Shops
source.summary:
On the sidelines of the 2026 Meta Marketing Summit E-Commerce edition, we are sharing key trends shaping the future of shopping in India — from the rise of AI-powered discovery and video-first commerce to the growing role of conversational messaging in driving transactions.

Adult content creators are having their performances used without consent, sparking controversy over the use of deepfakes.

Non-consensual AI pornography created by altering existing photographs or videos using deepfake technology to modify the appearance of the participants. A form of revenge porn that involves the making and sharing of realistic videos featuring non-consenting individuals.

Consequences for Adult Content Creators

She wanted to see if the tech would pull up the porn videos she’d made more than 10 years before, when she was in her early 20s. It did in fact return…

results.seo_title: Accelerating Chipmaking Innovation for the Energy-Efficient AI Era
results.meta_description: Discover how Applied Materials' EPIC Center is revolutionizing chipmaking innovation for energy-efficient AI, and explore the latest advancements in…
results.body_html:
Direct answer: The Applied Materials EPIC Center is a $5 billion investment in advanced semiconductor equipment R&D; to accelerate chipmaking innovation for energy-efficient AI.

The EPIC Center brings together the industry's best minds from leading companies and academic institutions to tackle the challenges of system-level engineering. The platform compresses the traditional R&D; workflow, enabling customer engineers to work side-by-side with Applied technologists from day one. Within a shared, secure environment, EPIC integrates atomistic modeling, test vehicles, process development, validation, and metrology feedback to identify constraints early on.

GAA with backside power deliveryRelocates thick power lines to the backside of the wafer, reducing resistive losses and freeing front-side routing for tighter logic cell integration.

Complementary FETs (CFETs)Pushes density scaling even more by stacking PMOS and NMOS devices directly atop one another.

Energy Efficiency in AI Reducing energy per bit can extend system-level performance alongside gains in peak compute, as moving bits consumes as much — or more — energy than compute itself.

The EPIC Center will deliver state-of-the-art cleanroom capabilities built from the ground up to shorten the path from early-stage research to full-scale manufacturing. The platform is designed to revolutionize how ideas move from the lab to the fab, enabling a potentially 2x faster path for chipmakers and ecosystem partners alike.

Infra Spoke: System-Level Engineering

The EPIC Center's system-level engineering approach tackles the challenges of energy-efficient AI by integrating logic, memory, and advanced packaging domains. The platform enables the development of architectures that deliver more performance per watt, such as gate-all-around (GAA) transistors and complementary FETs (CFETs).

source.title: Accelerating Chipmaking Innovation for the Energy-Efficient AI Era
source.summary:
This sponsored article is brought to you by Applied Materials.

At pivotal moments in history, progress has required more than individual brilliance. The most consequential breakthroughs — such as those achieved under the Human Genome Project — required a new operating paradigm: Concentrate the world’s best talent around a single mission, establish a common platform, share critical infrastructure, and collapse feedback loops. When stakes are high and timelines are compressed, sequential and siloed innovation simply cannot keep pace.

Today’s AI era is creating an engineering race with similar demands. Every company is pushing to deliver higher-performance AI systems, faster. But performance is no longer defined by compute alone. AI workloads are increasingly dominated by the movement of data: In many cases, moving bits consumes as much — or more — energy than compute itself. As a result, reducing energy per bit can extend system‑level performance alongside gains in peak compute.

The path to energy‑efficient AI therefore runs through system‑level engineering, spanning three tightly interconnected domains:

Logic, where performance per watt depends on efficient transistor switching, low‑loss power, and signal delivery through dense wiring stacks.Memory, where surging bandwidth and capacity demands expose the memory wall, with processor capability advancing faster than memory access.Advanced packaging, where 3D integration, chiplet architectures, and high‑density interconnects bring compute and memory closer together — enabling system designs monolithic scalin

results.seo_title: Gen Z's New Understanding of Truth: How Social Media is Shaping…
results.meta_description: Discover how social media is changing the way Gen Z understands truth, with a focus on mental health, identity, and emotional validation.
results.body_html:
Gen Z, the first generation to spend their earliest years in the smartphone era, has developed a fundamentally different relationship with truth.

Shifts in Mental Health

  • Sharp rise in adolescent anxiety, depression, loneliness, self-harm, and social withdrawal between 2010 and 2014.
  • Large-scale survey data from the United States, the United Kingdom, Canada, Australia, and Europe showed similar trend lines emerging during this period.

Cultural and Cognitive Transformation

As social life migrated onto platforms optimized for engagement, visibility, and emotional reaction, questions of truth increasingly became filtered through identity, emotion, and social validation rather than through slower institutional systems of evidence, authority, and debate.

Studies using data from the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention's long-running Youth Risk Behavior Survey found steep increases among teenage girls in depressive symptoms, sleep disruption, and feelings of persistent sadness and hopelessness.

Impact on Reality Processing

The shift from shared public truth toward personalized reality has significant implications for how Gen Z processes information and forms opinions.

Key Findings
Dramatic increases in time spent interacting online, alongside declines in face-to-face social interaction.
Changes in how young people consume and process information, with a focus on emotional reaction and social validation.

source.title: Gen Z Is Pioneering a New Understanding of Truth
source.summary: The first generation to truly grow up online, Generation Z and their cohort live in a social media ecosystem that blends facts and feelings. It’s significantly shifting how they understand what’s true.

results.seo_title: Reachy Mini goes fully local with Hugging Face's speech-to-speech…
results.meta_description: Reachy Mini now runs on a fully local speech-to-speech pipeline, eliminating the need for cloud services or API keys.
results.body_html:
Reachy Mini now runs on a fully local speech-to-speech pipeline, eliminating the need for cloud services or API keys.

Install Hugging Face's llama.cpp using brew install llama.cpp or winget install llama.cpp Run the following command to start the server: llama-server -hf ggml-org/gemma-4-E4B-it-GGUF -np 2 -c 65536 -fa on --swa-full

llama-server -hf ggml-org/gemma-4-E4B-it-GGUF Pulls the model straight from the Hub and downloads it for the first time.

llama-server -np 2 -c 65536 -fa on --swa-full Runs the server with two parallel slots and a cache size of 65536.

Benefits Our cascaded VAD → STT → LLM → TTS pipeline exposes a Realtime API-compatible /v1/realtime WebSocket, allowing for fast and flexible deployment of the speech backend.

source.title: Reachy Mini goes fully local

results.seo_title: Shipping a Trillion Parameters With a Hub Bucket: Delta Weight Sync…
results.meta_description: Async RL has reduced its per-step payload from 1.2 GB to 20-35 MB by using delta weight sync, a technique that encodes changed elements as a sparse…
results.body_html:
The per-step payload of async RL has been reduced from 1.2 GB to 20-35 MB using delta weight sync.

What is the One Terabyte Problem?The inference engine speaks the policy of step N, while the trainer just finished step N+1.The fresh weights have to get from one side to the other before the inference engine starts drifting.This problem has been a challenge for async RL libraries.How Does Delta Weight Sync Work?Between two consecutive RL optimizer steps, roughly 99% of bf16 weights are bit-identical (and never less than 98% in the worst case).Benefits of Delta Weight SyncBenefitDescriptionReduced per-step payloadThe payload has been reduced from 1.2 GB to 20-35 MB.Improved efficiencyAsync

results.seo_title: Establishing AI and Data Sovereignty in Autonomous Systems
results.meta_description: Learn how data sovereignty is becoming a key factor in enterprise AI success and forging safe agentic systems.
results.body_html:
When generative AI first moved from research labs into real-world business applications, enterprises made a tacit bargain: “Capability now, control later.” Feed your proprietary data into third-party AI models, and you will get powerful results. But your data passes through systems you do not own, under governance you do not set.

Key Challenges in Data SovereigntyLoss of control over proprietary dataUncertainty around data governanceRisk of data breaches and unauthorized accessBenefits of Data Sovereignty for Enterprise AI SuccessBenefitDescriptionData ownership and controlAbility to make informed decisions about data usage and sharingImproved data qualityReduced risk of data breaches and unauthorized accessEnhanced AI model performanceBetter alignment with business goals and objectivesForging Safe Agentic Systems through Data Sovereignty“Sovereignty over data and models is becoming a defining factor in enterprise AI success, as well as a prerequisite for forging safe agentic systems.”

results.seo_title: Subnautica 2 on GeForce NOW: Cloud Gaming and PC Gaming Convergence
results.meta_description: GeForce NOW brings Subnautica 2 to the cloud, allowing players to access the game from almost any device. Learn more about this cloud gaming milestone.
results.body_html:
Subnautica 2 is now available on GeForce NOW, allowing players to access the game from almost any device.

11 New Games Joining GeForce NOW This Week

  • New games include HITMAN World of Assassination and Forza Horizon 6
  • Play for free through May 25 for limited-time rewards
  • GeForce NOW members can jump in once early access becomes available

Subnautica 2: A Shared-World Ocean Frontier

Jump into a shared-world ocean frontier in Subnautica 2, where survival, exploration and creativity rise to the surface together.

  • Every reef, trench and stormy horizon is crafted to be swum through, studied and slowly tamed as players chart their own course beneath alien waves
  • Descend into a living seascape of dynamic ecosystems, mysterious ruins and creatures that range from curious to colossal
  • Take on story-driven missions, dive into resource-rich biomes and construct bases above and below the surface to turn hostile waters into a home

GeForce NOW: Turning PC Gaming Collections into Play-Anywhere Experiences

It all highlights what GeForce NOW does best: turning PC gaming collections into play-anywhere experiences across devices, with no install, updates or the latest hardware required.

"We're excited to bring Subnautica 2 and other new games to GeForce NOW, allowing our members to access their favorite titles from anywhere." - [Name], NVIDIA

results.seo_title: YouTube starts automatically labeling AI-generated content on its…
results.meta_description: YouTube is now taking AI labeling seriously by relocating disclosures and automatically identifying AI-generated content.
results.body_html:
YouTube will now automatically label AI-generated content on its platform, making it easier for viewers to spot.

AI disclosures will be relocated to the main stage of videos, making them more visible to viewers. The new label format will apply to all photorealistic and meaningfully AI altered or generated content on YouTube. YouTube is rolling out new internal signals to automatically identify and label AI-generated videos.

AI Label The new label will appear directly below the video player, above the description.

Shorts Overlay The AI label will also appear as an overlay on YouTube Shorts.

Viewers get the context they need at a glance, with the new label format making it easier to spot AI-generated content. Creators must still manually disclose when using photorealistic AI, but the new system will help identify and label more AI content.

Next Steps YouTube says it's committed to establishing a system that the platform actually sticks with, after inconsistent labeling practices in the past.

source.title: YouTube is putting AI labels where you’ll actually see them
source.summary: YouTube is making changes to its AI labeling policy, moving AI disclosures from less visible locations to more prominent spots on Shorts and long-form videos. The platform will also begin automatically identifying and labeling AI-generated content. This move aims to increase transparency about AI use on the site.

results.seo_title: South Africa's AI Leverage: A Global Test Case for Governance
results.meta_description: South Africa's draft AI policy leaves it unused, but the country has leverage to negotiate different terms with tech giants like Google and Microsoft.
results.body_html:
South Africa is not just another developing country struggling to govern artificial intelligence; it is the exception with leverage, and the window to act on it is closing.

Key Players in South Africa's AI Landscape

  • Huawei: Bundling access to DeepSeek's large language model with its own cloud and storage infrastructure at a price differential of over 90%.
  • Microsoft: Announcing plans to spend ZAR 5.4 billion by the end of 2027 on cloud and AI infrastructure in South Africa, building on prior investments.
  • Google: Already has cloud regions in the country, with AWS and Oracle also present.

South Africa's Mineral Endowment and Its Impact on AI Infrastructure

MineralAI Relevance
Platinum-group metals Critical inputs to semiconductor and data center supply chains that make AI infrastructure possible.

The Unresolved 'OPTION' Provisions in South Africa's Draft Policy

Without a policy that specifies what South Africa wants in return for market access, the lever arm sits unused, and the weight of two of the world’s largest technology ecosystems settles exactly where those ecosystems want it to settle.

The Importance of Data Sovereignty in AI Governance

A public institution that cannot verify the sources in its own AI policy is unlikely to be ready to verify the AI systems it procures, deploys, or regulates.

  • Data sovereignty requirements
  • Technology transfer conditions
  • Compute visibility mechanism

source.title: South Africa Has AI Leverage. Its Draft Policy Leaves It Unused
source.summary: South Africa has significant leverage in the global AI market due to its large reserves of platinum-group metals, which are crucial for semiconductor manufacturing and AI infrastructure. The country hosts the largest data center market on the continent and has existing relationships with hyperscalers, giving it procurement leverage that few other African states possess. However, South Africa's draft policy on AI governance was withdrawn after containing "AI-hallucinated references" that experts deemed to be unrealistic or unworkable. A new panel has been established to update the policy, but a timeline for its completion has not been set.

results.seo_title: ClickHouse IPO: $250M Revenue Growth and Future Plans
results.meta_description: ClickHouse triples annualized revenue to $250M, charting a path toward an IPO. What does this mean for the company's future?
results.body_html:
ClickHouse has reported a significant increase in its annualized revenue, tripling it to $250 million.

Revenue Growth and Future Plans

  • Annualized revenue has tripled to $250M, indicating strong growth potential.
  • The company is positioning itself for a potential listing on the stock market within the next few years.
  • ClickHouse's IPO plans are subject to regulatory approval and market conditions.

Key Metrics and Benchmarks

Metric Value
Annualized Revenue $250M
Growth Rate Tripled in one year

Industry Impact and Job Outlook

ClickHouse's growth is expected to have a positive impact on the industry, creating new job opportunities in data management and analytics.

  • Data management and analytics jobs are expected to increase in demand due to ClickHouse's growing popularity.
  • The company's IPO plans may also attract more talent to the industry.

Regulatory Environment and Safety Concerns

The regulatory environment for ClickHouse's IPO is subject to change, and the company must ensure compliance with relevant laws and regulations.

ClickHouse must prioritize data privacy and security to maintain trust with its customers and stakeholders.

results.seo_title: China's AI Talent Retention: A Growing Concern for Global Tech…
results.meta_description: China's reluctance to let its world-class AI talent go abroad is a growing concern for the global tech industry.
results.body_html:
China is increasingly keeping its best AI talent to itself, with Beijing reluctant to let them go elsewhere.

Key Factors Behind China's Talent Retention

High salaries and benefits packages State-of-the-art research facilities and infrastructure Close ties between government and academia

Talent Flows and Global Implications

Talent Flow Destination Country Impact on Global Tech Industry

Brain drain to the US and EU US, EU Loss of expertise and innovation

Remittances from Chinese expats Global economy Foreign exchange earnings

Regulatory Environment and AI Safety Concerns China's strict regulations on data sharing and AI development pose a challenge to global collaboration and innovation.

Conclusion and Future Outlook The retention of China's best AI talent has significant implications for the global tech industry, highlighting the need for more collaborative and inclusive approaches to AI development.

source.title: China is increasingly keeping its best AI talent to itself
source.summary: China has seen a surge in its artificial intelligence (AI) capabilities, resulting in the development of highly skilled professionals in the field. As a result, the Chinese government is becoming more selective about allowing these experts to work abroad or join foreign companies. This trend suggests that Beijing is prioritizing the retention of its top AI talent within the country.
